rationale: Elec. Code §254.183 governs campaign finance reporting thresholds for candidates and committees. It is wholly unrelated to the Tax Code §11.13(b) homestead exemption amount; no overlap exists.
quote: Sec. 254.183. MAXIMUM EXCEEDED. (a) An opposed candidate or specific-purpose committee that exceeds $500 in political contributions or political expenditures in the election shall file reports as required by Subchapter C or E, as applicable.
_note: No conflict: the cited election code provision concerns political contribution/expenditure reporting thresholds and has no relationship to property tax homestead exemption amounts.
